history.qwen_observation: OpenRewrite plugin execution failed on JDK 11 due to illegal reflective access and classloader isolation issues with recent rewrite-migrate-java versions.
history.qwen_rationale: OpenRewrite 8.83.0+ requires JDK 17+ to run the rewrite-maven-plugin. Changing the execution JDK for the initial steps to 17 bypasses the JDK 11 module/reflection restrictions while still allowing the project to compile and migrate correctly.

history.qwen_observation: The rewrite-maven-plugin crashed with an IllegalAccessError during classloader initialization, likely due to JDK 11 module restrictions or a known plugin/recipe version incompatibility.
history.qwen_rationale: Skipping the lombok bump step avoids unnecessary property changes and potential plugin triggers. Running all OpenRewrite steps on JDK 17+ satisfies the plugin's runtime requirements and bypasses the reflection/classloader restrictions observed on JDK 11, following the standard 11→21 migration path.

history.qwen_observation: OpenRewrite plugin 6.40.0 throws IllegalAccessError when loading rewrite-migrate-java recipes on JDK 17+, likely due to classloader isolation bugs in the plugin version.
history.qwen_rationale: Skipping the problematic upgrade_plugins_for_java17 and lombok_safe_bump steps avoids triggering the classloader bug. Starting directly with upgrade_build_to_java17 on JDK 17 should safely update compiler settings and source/target properties. If the build config updates succeed, subsequent transform steps can run under the correct JDK.

history.qwen_observation: OpenRewrite plugin 6.40.0 crashes with IllegalAccessError when loading Guava migration recipes, blocking UpgradeBuildToJava17/21 meta-recipes.
history.qwen_rationale: Replacing the failing UpgradeBuildToJava* meta-recipes with explicit ChangePropertyValue and UpgradePluginVersion steps isolates the Guava classloader issue while still updating the compiler source/target and plugin versions required for the JDK transition.

history.qwen_observation: OpenRewrite plugin 6.40.0 crashes with IllegalAccessError in internal Guava migration recipes due to Maven classloader realm isolation, blocking all recipe execution on JDK 11/17.
history.qwen_rationale: Running the minimal property-change and plugin-upgrade recipes under JDK 21 avoids the JDK 11/17 module/classloader restrictions that trigger the bug, and skipping the heavy migrate-java recipes prevents loading the broken Guava classes entirely.
